## Commemoration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_the_Baptist&action=edit&section=45 "Edit section: Commemoration")]

### Denominational festivals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_the_Baptist&action=edit&section=46 "Edit section: Denominational festivals")]

Christian festivals associated with Saint John the Baptist and Forerunner are celebrated at various days by different denominations and are dedicated to his conception, [birth](/wiki/Nativity_of_Saint_John_the_Baptist "Nativity of Saint John the Baptist"), and [death](/wiki/Beheading_of_Saint_John_the_Baptist "Beheading of Saint John the Baptist"), as well as in correlation to the [baptism of Jesus](/wiki/Baptism_of_Jesus "Baptism of Jesus"). The Eastern Church has feast days for the finding of his head (first, second, and third finding), as well as for his parents, Elizabeth and Zechariah. In the Russian Orthodox Church there is a feast day of the Transfer of the Right Hand of the Forerunner from Malta to Gatchina.

### Association with summer solstice

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_the_Baptist&action=edit&section=47 "Edit section: Association with summer solstice")]

The Feast of the nativity of Saint John closely coincides with the [June solstice](/wiki/June_solstice "June solstice"), also referred to as Midsummer in the Northern Hemisphere. The Christian holy day is fixed at 24 June; but in most countries festivities are mostly held the night before, on Saint John's Eve. "In England, 'Saint John's Tide' is combined with a midsummer celebration. Instead of the date of the summer solstice, they chose June 24. This may be because of the Baptist's own words, 'He must increase, but I must decrease' (John 3:30). John was, of course, referring to Jesus. John's day comes at the time when the sun is beginning to decrease..."[[233]](#cite_note-234)

### Patron saint and local festivals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_the_Baptist&action=edit&section=48 "Edit section: Patron saint and local festivals")]

Main category: [Saint John's Day](/wiki/Category:Saint_John%27s_Day "Category:Saint John's Day")

#### Middle East

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_the_Baptist&action=edit&section=49 "Edit section: Middle East")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/89/Ein_Kerem_Church_of_St_John_the_Baptist_by_David_Shankbone.jpg/250px-Ein_Kerem_Church_of_St_John_the_Baptist_by_David_Shankbone.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ein_Kerem_Church_of_St_John_the_Baptist_by_David_Shankbone.jpg)

Catholic church at his traditional birthplace in [Ein Kerem](/wiki/Ein_Kerem "Ein Kerem")

Saint John the Baptist's beheading is said to have taken place in [Machaerus](/wiki/Machaerus "Machaerus"), in central Jordan.[[234]](#cite_note-235)[[235]](#cite_note-236)[[236]](#cite_note-237)

#### Europe

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_the_Baptist&action=edit&section=50 "Edit section: Europe")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/09/St_John_the_Baptist_Titular_Statue_of_Xewkija_Gozo%2C_Malta.jpg/250px-St_John_the_Baptist_Titular_Statue_of_Xewkija_Gozo%2C_Malta.jpg)](/wiki/File:St_John_the_Baptist_Titular_Statue_of_Xewkija_Gozo,_Malta.jpg)

Wooden statue, [Pietro Paolo Azzopardi](/w/index.php?title=Pietro_Paolo_Azzopardi&action=edit&redlink=1 "Pietro Paolo Azzopardi (page does not exist)"), 1845, [Xewkija](/wiki/Xewkija "Xewkija")

In Spain, Saint John was venerated during the feast of the [Alhansara](/w/index.php?title=Alhansara&action=edit&redlink=1 "Alhansara (page does not exist)") in [Granada](/wiki/Granada "Granada"), and also in [Gaztelugatxe](/wiki/Gaztelugatxe "Gaztelugatxe")[[237]](#cite_note-238)

In the United Kingdom, Saint John is the patron of [Penzance](/wiki/Penzance "Penzance"), [Cornwall](/wiki/Cornwall "Cornwall"). In [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland"), he is the patron saint of [Perth](/wiki/Perth,_Scotland "Perth, Scotland"), which used to be known as St. John's Toun of Perth. The main church in the city is still the medieval Kirk of St. John the Baptist and the city's [professional](/wiki/Professional_sports "Professional sports") [football club](/wiki/Football_club "Football club") is called [St Johnstone F.C.](/wiki/St_Johnstone_F.C. "St Johnstone F.C.")

Also, on the night of 23 June on to the 24th, Saint John is celebrated as the patron saint of [Porto](/wiki/Porto "Porto"), the second largest city in [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal"). An article from June 2004 in *[The Guardian](/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")* remarked that "Porto's *[Festa de São João](/wiki/Festa_de_S%C3%A3o_Jo%C3%A3o "Festa de São João")* is one of Europe's liveliest street festivals, yet it is relatively unknown outside the country".[[238]](#cite_note-239)

As patron saint of the original Order of Knights of the Hospital of Saint John, he is the patron of the Knights Hospitaller of Jerusalem, [Malta](/wiki/Malta "Malta"), [Florence](/wiki/Florence "Florence"), [Cesena](/wiki/Cesena "Cesena"), [Turin](/wiki/Turin "Turin") and [Genoa](/wiki/Genoa "Genoa"), Italy; as well as of Malta as a whole and of [Xewkija](/wiki/Xewkija "Xewkija") and [Gozo](/wiki/Gozo "Gozo") in Malta, which remember him with a great feast on the Sunday nearest to 24 June.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

#### Americas

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_the_Baptist&action=edit&section=51 "Edit section: Americas")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/46/Decora%C3%A7%C3%A3o_S%C3%A3o_Jo%C3%A3o_%C3%A0_noite_%281136555435%29.jpg/250px-Decora%C3%A7%C3%A3o_S%C3%A3o_Jo%C3%A3o_%C3%A0_noite_%281136555435%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Decora%C3%A7%C3%A3o_S%C3%A3o_Jo%C3%A3o_%C3%A0_noite_(1136555435).jpg)

[Saint John Festival](/wiki/Festa_Junina "Festa Junina") in [Campina Grande](/wiki/Campina_Grande "Campina Grande"), [Paraíba](/wiki/Para%C3%ADba "Paraíba"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil").

[Saint John's Day](/wiki/Festa_Junina "Festa Junina"), celebrated on June 24, is one of the most important dates in [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil")’s traditional [June Festivals](/wiki/Festa_Junina "Festa Junina"), known as "Festas Juninas". These festivities are deeply rooted in the [country’s rural](/wiki/Caipira_culture "Caipira culture") heritage and [Catholic traditions](/wiki/Catholic_Church_in_Brazil "Catholic Church in Brazil"), honoring Saint John the Baptist. Across Brazil, especially in the [Northeast](/wiki/Northeast_Region,_Brazil "Northeast Region, Brazil"), cities like [Campina Grande](/wiki/Campina_Grande "Campina Grande") and [Caruaru](/wiki/Caruaru "Caruaru") host massive celebrations with [bonfires](/wiki/Bonfire "Bonfire"), "quadrilhas" ([square dance](/wiki/Square_dance "Square dance")), colorful customs, [fireworks](/wiki/Fireworks "Fireworks"), and [traditional foods](/wiki/Brazilian_cuisine "Brazilian cuisine"), specially made from [corn](/wiki/Maize "Maize") and [peanut](/wiki/Peanut "Peanut"), such as "[pamonha](/wiki/Pamonha "Pamonha")", "[canjica](/wiki/Canjica "Canjica")", and "[pé-de-moleque](/wiki/P%C3%A9_de_moleque "Pé de moleque")". In the [North](/wiki/North_Region,_Brazil "North Region, Brazil"), particularly in the state of [Amazonas](/wiki/Amazonas_(Brazilian_state) "Amazonas (Brazilian state)"), the "[Festival de Parintins](/wiki/Parintins_Folklore_Festival "Parintins Folklore Festival")" adds a unique dimension to the June celebrations with the "[Boi-Bumbá](/wiki/Bumba_Meu_Boi "Bumba Meu Boi")" folklore, perform theatrical retellings of [Amazonian](/wiki/Amazon_rainforest "Amazon rainforest") myths, mixing [indigenous](/wiki/Indigenous_peoples_in_Brazil "Indigenous peoples in Brazil"), [African](/wiki/Afro-Brazilians "Afro-Brazilians"), and [European](/wiki/White_Brazilians "White Brazilians") cultural elements. More than a religious observance, the "Festa de São João" represents a vibrant expression of [Brazilian folklore](/wiki/Brazilian_folklore "Brazilian folklore"), reinforcing communal bonds and celebrating the diverse cultural identities that shape the nation.

Saint John the Baptist is the [patron saint](/wiki/Patron_saint "Patron saint") of the [Commonwealth of Puerto Rico](/wiki/Commonwealth_of_Puerto_Rico "Commonwealth of Puerto Rico") and its capital city, [San Juan](/wiki/San_Juan,_Puerto_Rico "San Juan, Puerto Rico"). In 1521, the island was given its formal name, *"San Juan Bautista de Puerto Rico"*, following the custom of christening a town with its formal name and the name which [Christopher Columbus](/wiki/Christopher_Columbus "Christopher Columbus") had originally given the island. The names *"San Juan Bautista"* and *"Puerto Rico"* were eventually used in reference to both city and island, leading to a reversal in terminology by most inhabitants largely due to a [cartographic](/wiki/Cartographic "Cartographic") error. By 1746, the city's name (*"Puerto Rico"*) had become that of the entire island, while the name for the island (*"San Juan Bautista"*) had become that of the city. The [official motto](/wiki/List_of_U.S._state_mottos "List of U.S. state mottos") of Puerto Rico also references the saint: *Joannes Est Nomen Eius*.[[239]](#cite_note-240)[[240]](#cite_note-241)

He is also a patron saint of [French Canada](/wiki/French_Canada "French Canada") and [Newfoundland](/wiki/Newfoundland "Newfoundland"). The Canadian cities of [St. John's, Newfoundland](/wiki/St._John%27s,_Newfoundland "St. John's, Newfoundland") (1497), [Saint John, New Brunswick](/wiki/Saint_John,_New_Brunswick "Saint John, New Brunswick") (1604), and [Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu](/wiki/Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu "Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu"), Quebec (1665), were all named in his honour. His feast day of 24 June is celebrated officially in [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec") as the *[Fête Nationale du Québec](/wiki/F%C3%AAte_Nationale_du_Qu%C3%A9bec "Fête Nationale du Québec")* and was previously celebrated in Newfoundland as [Discovery Day](/wiki/Discovery_Day "Discovery Day").[[241]](#cite_note-242)

He is also patron of the [Roman Catholic Diocese of Charleston](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Charleston "Roman Catholic Diocese of Charleston"), which covers the whole of [South Carolina](/wiki/South_Carolina "South Carolina") in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States").[[242]](#cite_note-243)

#### Southeast Asia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_the_Baptist&action=edit&section=52 "Edit section: Southeast Asia")]

[Calamba, Laguna](/wiki/Calamba,_Laguna "Calamba, Laguna"), [Calumpit, Bulacan](/wiki/Calumpit,_Bulacan "Calumpit, Bulacan"), [Balayan](/wiki/Balayan "Balayan"), and [Lian](/wiki/Lian,_Batangas "Lian, Batangas") in [Batangas](/wiki/Batangas "Batangas"), [Sipocot](/wiki/Sipocot "Sipocot"), and [San Fernando](/wiki/San_Fernando,_Camarines_Sur "San Fernando, Camarines Sur") in [Camarines Sur](/wiki/Camarines_Sur "Camarines Sur"), [Daet, Camarines Norte](/wiki/Daet,_Camarines_Norte "Daet, Camarines Norte"), [San Juan, Metro Manila](/wiki/San_Juan,_Metro_Manila "San Juan, Metro Manila"), [Tabuelan, Cebu](/wiki/Tabuelan,_Cebu "Tabuelan, Cebu"), [Jimenez, Misamis Occidental](/wiki/Jimenez,_Misamis_Occidental "Jimenez, Misamis Occidental"), [Badiangan](/wiki/Badiangan "Badiangan"), [Banate](/wiki/Banate,_Iloilo "Banate, Iloilo"), [Dingle](/wiki/Dingle,_Iloilo "Dingle, Iloilo"), [Igbaras](/wiki/Igbaras "Igbaras"), and [Sara](/wiki/Sara,_Iloilo "Sara, Iloilo") in [Iloilo](/wiki/Iloilo "Iloilo") and the oldest in [Taytay, Rizal](/wiki/Taytay,_Rizal "Taytay, Rizal") are among several places in the [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines") that venerate John as the town or city patron. A common practise of many [Filipino fiestas](/wiki/Culture_of_the_Philippines "Culture of the Philippines") in his honour is bathing and the dousing of people in memory of John's iconic act. The custom is similar in form to [Songkran](/wiki/Songkran "Songkran") and [Holi](/wiki/Holi "Holi"), and serves as a playful respite from the intense tropical heat. While famed for the [Black Nazarene](/wiki/Black_Nazarene "Black Nazarene") it enshrines, [Quiapo Church](/wiki/Quiapo_Church "Quiapo Church") in [Manila](/wiki/Manila "Manila") actually has Saint John as its patron saint.[[243]](#cite_note-244)

### Orders and societies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_the_Baptist&action=edit&section=53 "Edit section: Orders and societies")]

A number of [religious orders](/wiki/Religious_orders "Religious orders") who include or have included in their name a mention of John the Baptist have been called [Baptistines](/wiki/Baptistines_(disambiguation) "Baptistines (disambiguation)").

John the Baptist is the name-giving patron of the [Knights Hospitaller](/wiki/Knights_Hospitaller "Knights Hospitaller"), or also called Knights of Saint John.[[244]](#cite_note-245)

Along with [John the Evangelist](/wiki/John_the_Evangelist "John the Evangelist"), John the Baptist is claimed as a patron saint by the fraternal society of [Freemasons](/wiki/Freemasons "Freemasons").[[245]](#cite_note-246)
